title: lichen_l2.c:527-529 comment explains defensive copy but is verbose
description: The comment block at lichen_l2.c:521-529 explaining why rx_link_key is copied rather than pointer-captured is accurate but verbose (9 lines). The code pattern (copy + local pointer) is idiomatic for key material handling. Trim to: '/* SECURITY: Copy key to stack to survive hypothetical cleanup reordering. */'
close_reason: Trimmed verbose ownership comment from 7 lines to 2: 'net_recv_data takes ownership on success; we unref on failure'
